info.title: ⏱️ ChronoTrack: Precision HR & Workforce Geofencing API
info.description:
### 🌐 Overview
**ChronoTrack** is an enterprise-grade workforce management engine designed for high-stakes industrial and corporate environments. It bridges the gap between physical office presence and digital payroll accuracy using advanced Geospatial validation.

#### 🚀 Core System Capabilities
* **Precision Attendance Engine**: A Geofencing-validated check-in system that cross-references **GPS coordinates** and branch perimeters for millisecond-accurate logs.
* **Automated Governance**: Powered by **Hangfire**, the system runs autonomous background audits to flag missing check-outs and calculate late penalties in real-time.
* **Compensation Intelligence**: A robust financial matrix that calculates net pay by aggregating base salaries, overtime incentives, and lateness deductions.
* **Architectural Integrity**: Implements the **Unit of Work** and **Generic Repository** patterns for atomic database transactions and high-performance querying.

#### 🔑 Security & Identity
Secured via **ASP.NET Identity Core** and **JWT Bearer Authentication**. All administrative and financial endpoints enforce strict **Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)** to ensure data privacy and fiscal security.

> **Engineering Note:** All geospatial calculations utilize the **Haversine Formula** to ensure accuracy across global branch networks.

post.description:
### 🔐 Employee Access Gateway

This endpoint validates employee credentials and returns a comprehensive profile payload required for mobile and web applications.

#### **⚙️ Internal Business Logic:**
| Feature | Logic |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Auto-Admin Bootstrapping** | If the configured Admin Email is not found in the DB but matches the request, the system **automatically creates** a default Admin record. |
| **Geo-Fencing Data** | Returns Branch `Latitude`, `Longitude`, and `Radius` to enable location-based attendance checking. |
| **Shift Context** | Synchronizes employee shift timings to enforce late-arrival and early-departure rules. |

#### **📋 Security Context:**
* **JWT Generation**: Returns a signed token including Role claims.
* **Stateless Auth**: The token must be included in the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header for all secure endpoints.

example.message: Login Successful
example.name: Ahmed Ali

example.message: You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

post.description:
### 📧 Secure Password Reset Trigger

Sends a secure, time-sensitive reset token to the employee's registered email address. This is the first step in the automated recovery workflow.

#### **⚙️ Recovery Logic Workflow:**
| Step | Action | Logic |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **1. Identity Check** | User Lookup | Verifies the email exists in the **ASP.NET Identity** store. |
| **2. Token Generation** | Security Token | Generates a unique `PasswordResetToken` for the user. |
| **3. Encoding** | URL Safety | Encodes the token using `HttpUtility.UrlEncode` to prevent transport errors. |
| **4. Notification** | SMTP Service | Dispatching a recovery email containing the token through the **Email Service**. |

#### **⚠️ Potential Failure Points:**
* **Unregistered Email**: Returns a **404 Not Found** with a 'not registered' message.
* **SMTP Failure**: If the email service is down, a **500 Internal Server Error** is logged and returned.

application/json.example: Forget password request completed successfully for email: k.ayman@hrsystem.com

example.message: You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

post.summary: Complete password recovery and initialize session
post.description:
### 🔑 Secure Identity Restoration

Finalizes the password recovery workflow by validating the security token and updating the account credentials. Upon success, the system automatically authenticates the user.

#### **⚙️ Atomic Fulfillment Logic:**
| Action | Logic |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Token Validation** | Verifies the token against the **ASP.NET Identity** security stamp. |
| **Credential Update** | Replaces the old password hash with the new one. |
| **Instant Auth** | Generates a new **JWT Token** and fetches the employee's `Shift` and `Branch` data immediately. |

#### **⚠️ Critical Security Notes:**
* **One-Time Use**: The reset token is invalidated immediately after a successful reset.
* **Password Policy**: New passwords must comply with the system's complexity requirements (Length, Non-alphanumeric, etc.).

example.message: Password Reset Successful
example.name: Ahmed Ali

example.message: You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

get.summary: Retrieve a comprehensive list of all company branches
get.description:
### 🌍 Global Location Directory

Fetches all registered branches with their complete spatial configuration. This data is critical for assigning employees to locations and validating GPS check-ins.

#### **⚙️ Operational Insights:**
| Attribute | Business Logic |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Spatial Data** | Provides `Latitude` and `Longitude` for map rendering. |
| **Radius Guard** | Defines the physical boundary (in meters) for valid attendance via the mobile app. |
| **Access Control** | Strictly limited to **Admin** role for organizational oversight. |

#### **🚨 Error Handling:**
* **500 Internal Error**: Returned if the database fails or the service layer returns a null reference.

example.message: You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.
An error occurred while retrieving branches.

get.summary: Retrieve a specific branch profile by ID
get.description:
### 📍 Precision Location Discovery

Fetches a single branch's metadata and geospatial configuration. This profile is essential for mobile clients to initialize regional **Geo-fencing** parameters.

#### **⚙️ Data Mapping Intelligence:**
| Feature | Technical Application |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Spatial Coordinates** | Returns `Latitude` and `Longitude` for precise GPS anchoring. |
| **Boundary Definition** | Returns the `Radius` (in meters) which defines the valid attendance perimeter. |
| **ID Validation** | Enforces a positive integer constraint at the service layer to prevent invalid queries. |

#### **🔐 Security Context:**
* **Authorized Access**: Open to both `Admin` and `User` roles to allow employees to see their assigned branch details.
* **Error Handling**: Returns a 404 status if the requested ID does not correspond to an active branch record.

example.message: Branch with ID 10 not found.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

post.summary: Initialize a new physical branch location
post.description:
### 🏗️ Infrastructure Provisioning

Registers a new corporate branch into the HRSystem. This operation defines the geographic 'Safe Zone' where employees are permitted to record attendance.

#### **⚙️ Provisioning Logic:**
| Stage | Action | Purpose |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **1. Validation** | Model State Check | Ensures `Latitude`/`Longitude` fall within global GPS bounds and name is unique. |
| **2. Persistence** | DB Insertion | Commits the spatial configuration to the `Branches` table. |
| **3. Response** | Resource Mapping | Returns a `201 Created` status with the newly assigned Database ID. |

#### **⚠️ Geo-fencing Requirements:**
* **Radius Precision**: The `Radius` must be sufficient to account for GPS drift (recommended: 50m - 200m).
* **Authorization**: Strictly limited to **Admin** role for organizational infrastructure management.

example.message: You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

put.description:
### 🔄 Spatial Data Synchronization

Modifies an existing branch's administrative name or geographic Geo-fencing parameters. This endpoint is critical for adjusting operational boundaries as office locations evolve.

#### **⚙️ Update Logic (Delta Mapping):**
| Field Status | Service Action |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Provided Value** | Overwrites the existing value in the database. |
| **Null / Empty** | Ignored; the original database value is preserved. |

#### **⚠️ Operational Guards:**
* **Existence Check**: Returns a 404 if the provided `Id` does not match any active record.
* **ID Validation**: Strictly requires a positive integer `Id` > 0.
* **Consistency**: Updating spatial coordinates (`Latitude`/`Longitude`) will immediately affect all employees assigned to this branch for attendance validation.

example.message: Successfully updated branch
Branch with ID 99 not found.
An error occurred while updating the branch.
example.error: Branch ID must be a positive integer.

delete.description:
### ⚠️ Infrastructure Decommissioning

Permanently deletes a physical branch from the organizational structure. This action is irreversible and should be performed with caution.

#### **⚙️ Operational Workflow:**
| Stage | Action | Purpose |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **1. Validation** | Positive ID Check | Ensures the ID is greater than zero before hitting the database. |
| **2. Existence Check** | Soft Inquiry | Verifies the record exists in the `Branches` table. |
| **3. Execution** | Hard Delete | Removes the record and commits the change via Unit of Work. |

#### **🚨 Critical Impact:**
* **Data Integrity**: Deleting a branch that still has assigned employees may cause foreign key constraint violations if not handled by the database schema.
* **Authorization**: Restricted to **Admin** role only.

example.message: Branch with ID 10 not found.
An error occurred while deleting the branch.
example.error: Branch ID must be a positive integer.

post.description:
### 🔐 Security Privilege Provisioning

Adds a new administrative or functional role to the system's Identity store. This role can later be assigned to employees to control access to specific endpoints.

#### **⚙️ Role Creation Logic:**
| Phase | Action | Purpose |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **1. Normalization** | Case-Insensitive Check | Identity Core ensures 'Admin' and 'admin' are treated as duplicates. |
| **2. Validation** | Integrity Check | Verifies the role name is not null and meets system length requirements. |
| **3. Persistence** | Store Sync | Commits the new role to the `AspNetRoles` table. |

#### **⚠️ Constraints:**
* **Admin Only**: This operation requires an active administrative session.
* **Uniqueness**: Role names must be unique system-wide.

example.message: Role created successfully.
Error creating role. Role may already exist or invalid data provided.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

get.summary: Retrieve a comprehensive list of all system roles
get.description:
### 📜 Global Role Directory

Fetches all defined security roles from the **ASP.NET Identity** store. This list is primarily used for administrative mapping and privilege assignment.

#### **⚙️ Retrieval Intelligence:**
| Logic | Description |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Data Transformation** | Automatically maps internal `IdentityRole` entities to light-weight `RoleDTO` objects. |
| **Empty Handling** | If no roles are configured, the system returns an empty array instead of a 404, ensuring frontend stability. |
| **Security Boundary** | Accessible strictly by users with the **Admin** role to prevent exposure of security hierarchy. |

#### **⚠️ Response Guidance:**
* **Success (200)**: Look into the `Data` field for the list of roles.
* **Unauthorized (401)**: Triggered if the JWT token is missing or expired.

example.message: Roles retrieved successfully.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

get.summary: Retrieve a specific security role profile
get.description:
### 🔍 Target Privilege Discovery

Fetches the full metadata of a security role identified by its unique GUID. This endpoint is vital for ensuring consistency before modifying security policies.

#### **⚙️ Retrieval Intelligence:**
| Factor | Technical Logic |
| :--- | :--- |
| **ID Validation** | The service layer enforces a strict non-empty string check to prevent malformed queries. |
| **Identity Store Sync** | Directly queries the `AspNetRoles` table using the built-in `FindByIdAsync` manager. |
| **Consistency Guard** | Returns a clear 404 response if the ID is formatted correctly but does not exist in the store. |

#### **🔐 Security Context:**
* **Authorization**: Strictly limited to **Admin** role to prevent leakage of the internal security structure.
* **Input**: Expects a string-based ID (typically a GUID) in the URL path.

example.message: Role retrieved successfully.
No role found with ID role-999.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

put.summary: Modify an existing security role's name
put.description:
### 🔄 Role Metadata Synchronization

Updates the descriptive name of a security role in the `AspNetRoles` store. This operation is non-destructive to user assignments as it preserves the original Role GUID.

#### **⚙️ Update Intelligence:**
| Logic | Description |
| :--- | :--- |
| **GUID Consistency** | Ensures that only the name is changed, maintaining all existing employee-to-role relationships. |
| **Normalization** | The Identity Manager automatically handles name normalization to ensure system-wide consistency. |
| **Validation Guard** | Rejects requests with null or empty IDs/Names before reaching the database layer. |

#### **⚠️ Critical Impact:**
* **Naming Conflicts**: The operation will fail if the new `RoleName` matches another existing role.
* **Permissions**: Strictly restricted to the **Admin** role.

example.message: Role updated successfully.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.
No role found with ID e2b1-role-id or error updating role.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

delete.description:
### ⚠️ Irreversible Privilege Removal

Deletes a specific security role from the `AspNetRoles` store. This action is irreversible and should be performed only when the role is no longer assigned to any users.

#### **⚙️ Operational Workflow:**
| Stage | Action | Purpose |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **1. Identity Check** | `FindByIdAsync` | Verifies the role exists before attempting deletion. |
| **2. Dependency Check** | System Integrity | Identity Core internally validates if the role can be safely removed. |
| **3. Finalization** | Hard Delete | Removes the record from the database and clears the security cache. |

#### **🚨 Critical Impact:**
* **User Assignment**: If the role is currently assigned to users, the deletion may fail depending on the database's foreign key constraints.
* **Access Loss**: Users currently relying on this role for access will lose those permissions immediately upon deletion.

example.message: Role deleted successfully.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.
No role found with ID role-uuid-999 or error deleting role.
An error occurred while deleting the role.
example.error: Role ID cannot be null or empty.

post.description:
### 🔐 Security Role Synchronization

This endpoint performs an **Atomic Override** of an employee's security privileges. It clears all existing roles and assigns a new specified set.

#### **⚙️ Synchronization Logic:**
| Step | Action | Description |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **1. Identity Check** | User & Role Validation | Ensures the `UserId` exists and all `RoleNames` are defined in the system. |
| **2. Clean Slate** | Role Purge | Executes `RemoveFromRolesAsync` to wipe any previous assignments. |
| **3. Re-assignment** | Role Injection | Executes `AddToRolesAsync` to apply the new security context. |

#### **⚠️ Critical Rules:**
* **Partial Failure**: If role removal fails, the new roles will not be added to maintain consistency.
* **JWT Refresh**: The affected user may need to re-login to reflect role changes in their JWT claims.

example.message: User with ID 'emp-uuid-550' added to roles 'Admin, HR_Manager' successfully.
The following roles do not exist: SuperAdmin.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

delete.summary: Revoke a specific security role from an employee
delete.description:
### 🛡️ Granular Privilege Revocation

Removes a single specified role from an employee's profile while preserving their other existing permissions. This is a critical operation for internal role transitions.

#### **⚙️ Revocation Workflow:**
| Phase | Action | Purpose |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **1. Identity Check** | `FindByIdAsync` | Confirms the target employee exists in the system. |
| **2. Role Verification** | `RoleExistsAsync` | Confirms the specified role name is valid and active. |
| **3. Membership Audit** | `IsInRoleAsync` | Prevents redundant operations if the user doesn't possess the role. |
| **4. Execution** | `RemoveFromRoleAsync` | Safely detaches the role from the user in the `AspNetUserRoles` table. |

#### **⚠️ Security Notes:**
* **Real-time Impact**: The change is effective immediately in the database, but active sessions may require a token refresh.
* **Admin Privileges**: Only users with elevated `Admin` roles can perform revocation.

example.message: User with ID 'user-123' removed from role 'Editor' successfully.
User with ID 'user-123' is not in role 'Admin'.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

post.summary: Register and onboard a new employee
post.description:
### 🚀 Automated Employee Onboarding

This endpoint orchestrates a full onboarding workflow for new personnel. It ensures that the employee account is not only created but also fully configured for immediate use.

#### **⚙️ Atomic Provisioning Workflow:**
| Phase | Action | Logic |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **1. Identity** | Account Creation | Generates an **ASP.NET Identity** user with secure password hashing. |
| **2. Role Assignment** | Authorization | Automatically assigns the `User` role (or `Admin` if name is 'Admin'). |
| **3. Scheduling** | Default Shift | Creates an initial **9:00 AM - 5:00 PM** shift entry automatically. |
| **4. Integrity** | Transaction Guard | Wraps all steps in a DB transaction; fails all if one step fails. |

#### **⚠️ Validation Rules:**
* **Uniqueness**: Rejects duplicate `Email` or `Name` (Username).
* **Geographical Linking**: Must provide a valid `BranchId` for attendance tracking.

example.message: User created successfully!
User with this email already exists.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

get.summary: Search and paginate through employee directory
get.description:
### 🔍 Advanced Workforce Discovery

Retrieves a paginated list of employees with support for multi-criteria filtering. This endpoint is designed for administrative dashboards to manage large workforce datasets efficiently.

#### **⚙️ Search & Hydration Logic:**
| Feature | Technical Implementation |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Flexible Filtering** | Uses an **'OR' logic sequence** across `Name`, `Email`, `NationalId`, and more. If multiple filters are provided, any match will return the record. |
| **Data Hydration** | For every user found, the system performs sub-queries to fetch their active **Shifts**, **Branch details**, and **System Roles**. |
| **Pagination** | Implements `.Skip()` and `.Take()` at the database level for optimized performance. |

#### **📋 Query Capabilities:**
* **Partial Matches**: `Name`, `Email`, and `PhoneNumber` support partial string matching (`.Contains()`).
* **Exact Matches**: `Gender`, `Salary`, and `Dates` (Hiring/Birth) require exact values.

> **Pro Tip:** If no filters are provided, the system returns all users sorted alphabetically by Name.

parameters.name: PageNumber
parameters.description: The page index to retrieve. Starts from 1.

parameters.name: PageSize
parameters.description: Number of records per page.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: Filter by full or partial employee name.

parameters.name: Email
parameters.description: Filter by full or partial email address.

parameters.name: PhoneNumber
parameters.description: Filter by mobile or phone number.

parameters.name: NationalId
parameters.description: Filter by unique 14-digit National ID.

parameters.name: Gender
parameters.description: Filter by Gender (e.g., Male or Female).

parameters.name: HiringDate
parameters.description: Filter by exact hiring date.

parameters.name: DateOfBarth
parameters.description: Filter by exact date of birth.

parameters.name: Salary
parameters.description: Filter by exact base salary amount.

example.message: No users found.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.
An error occurred while retrieving users.
example.error: Timeout occurred while querying the database

get.summary: Fetch comprehensive employee profile by Identity ID
get.description:
### 👤 Unified Profile Retrieval

Retrieves a detailed employee dossier combining identity credentials, geographic branch assignments, and scheduled work shifts into a single payload.

#### **⚙️ Profile Reconstruction Logic:**
| Data Source | Logic Applied |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Identity Store** | Fetches core attributes (`Email`, `Name`, `Salary`) from ASP.NET Identity. |
| **Shift Resolver** | Maps and formats active assignments from the `EmployeeShifts` junction table to `H:mm` format. |
| **Branch Locator** | Hydrates the profile with current Geo-fencing coordinates and allowed `Radius`. |

#### **🔐 Security Policy:**
* **Role Access**: Restricted to `Admin` for organizational auditing or `User` for self-profile retrieval.
* **Fallback Logic**: If no branch or shifts are assigned, the respective objects will return `null` or an empty list without failing the request.

example.message: User found
User with ID '12345' not found.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

get.summary: Retrieve all employees assigned to a specific branch
get.description:
### 🏢 Branch-Level Workforce Retrieval

Fetches a comprehensive list of all personnel linked to a specific organizational unit (Branch). This endpoint is essential for branch managers to monitor their local workforce and schedules.

#### **⚙️ Retrieval & Mapping Engine:**
| Process | Description |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Branch Filtering** | Performs an optimized SQL query to filter the `AspNetUsers` store by `BranchId`. |
| **Deep Hydration** | For each employee found, the system resolves their specific **Shifts** (formatted to `H:mm`) and confirms their **Branch details**. |
| **Role Resolution** | Asynchronously fetches assigned system roles for every user in the list. |

#### **⚠️ Constraints & Validation:**
* **ID Validation**: Rejects `BranchId` values less than or equal to zero (400 Bad Request).
* **Empty State**: Returns a 404 Not Found if the branch exists but currently has no assigned employees.

example.message: No employees found for BranchId: 5
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

get.summary: Retrieve detailed vacation consumption for an employee
get.description:
### 🌴 Vacation Analytics Engine

Calculates and retrieves the total vacation days consumed by an employee within a specific month and year. The engine dynamically converts logged hours into 'Work Days' based on the system's global settings.

#### **⚙️ Calculation Logic:**
| Factor | Logic Applied |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Hour-to-Day Ratio** | Divides total vacation hours by `NumberOfDayWorkingHours` defined in **General Settings**. |
| **Time-Bound Filter** | Automatically defaults to the **Current Month/Year** if not explicitly provided in the query string. |
| **Data Aggregation** | Groups all vacation entries for the period to provide a summarized `TotalVacationDays` count. |

#### **⚠️ Operational Constraints:**
* **Dependency**: Requires `GeneralSettings` to be configured; otherwise, the calculation will fail.
* **Localization**: Dates in the details list are formatted as `yyyy-MM-dd` for frontend consistency.

parameters.name: PageNumber
parameters.description: Page number for results pagination. Starts at 1.

parameters.name: PageSize
parameters.description: Maximum number of employee records per page.

parameters.name: Year
parameters.description: Filter records by a specific Year. Defaults to Current Year if null.

parameters.name: Month
parameters.description: Filter records by a specific Month (1-12). Defaults to Current Month if null.

example.message: No vacation records found for EmployeeId: emp-123
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

get.summary: Generate a global vacation consumption report for all staff
get.description:
### 📊 Organizational Vacation Audit

Generates a high-level summary of vacation usage across the entire workforce. It calculates the total days taken by each employee within the specified billing month.

#### **⚙️ Reporting Intelligence:**
| Feature | Logic | Purpose |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Cross-Reference** | Matches `EmployeeVacation` logs with `AspNetUsers` profiles. | Validates record ownership and display names. |
| **Dynamic Ratio** | Uses `GeneralSettings.NumberOfDayWorkingHours` for conversion. | Ensures calculations respect the latest corporate policy. |
| **Smart Pagination** | Filters records in memory first, then paginates the resulting unique employee list. | Optimizes DB performance for heavy reporting months. |

#### **🔐 Admin Constraints:**
* **Authorization**: Strictly limited to the `Admin` role.
* **Period Defaulting**: If `Month`/`Year` are omitted, the report automatically generates for the **current calendar month**.

parameters.name: PageNumber
parameters.description: Page number for results pagination. Starts at 1.

parameters.name: PageSize
parameters.description: Maximum number of employee records per page.

parameters.name: Year
parameters.description: Filter records by a specific Year. Defaults to Current Year if null.

parameters.name: Month
parameters.description: Filter records by a specific Month (1-12). Defaults to Current Month if null.

example.message: No vacation records found for any employee.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

get.summary: Retrieve a global net salary report for all employees
get.description:
### 💰 Organizational Payroll Summary

Fetches the pre-calculated net salary records for all employees within a specific fiscal month and year. This data is pulled from the finalized `EmployeeSalaries` ledger.

#### **⚙️ Reporting Engine Logic:**
| Process | Description |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Date Framing** | Automatically generates a start-to-end date range for the provided `Month`/`Year`. |
| **Null Handling** | If no period is specified, the system defaults to the **current calendar month**. |
| **Resource Mapping** | Joins salary logs with the `Users` table to resolve employee names. |

#### **📋 Pagination & Sorting:**
* **Order**: Results are sorted alphabetically by `EmployeeName` to facilitate manual auditing.
* **Page Control**: Efficiently skips and takes records at the SQL level using `ParamDTO` inputs.

parameters.name: PageNumber
parameters.description: Page number for results pagination. Starts at 1.

parameters.name: PageSize
parameters.description: Maximum number of employee records per page.

parameters.name: Year
parameters.description: Filter records by a specific Year. Defaults to Current Year if null.

parameters.name: Month
parameters.description: Filter records by a specific Month (1-12). Defaults to Current Month if null.

example.message: No salary records found for any employee.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

get.summary: Generate a comprehensive salary breakdown for an employee
get.description:
### 💳 Payroll Audit & Analysis Engine

Calculates a detailed real-time payroll slip for an employee by auditing their monthly performance against corporate financial rules.

#### **⚠️ Potential Errors:**
| Status Code | Scenario | Reason |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **404 Not Found** | **Employee Missing** | The provided `EmployeeId` does not exist in the database. |
| **500 Server Error** | **Settings Missing** | The system could not find `GeneralSettings` to calculate rates. |

#### **⚙️ Calculation Logic:**
* **Base Rate**: Derived from `BaseSalary` divided by effective working days.
* **Deductions**: Calculated from late hours and unauthorized absence logs.
* **Bonuses**: Includes Overtime, Sales Percentages, and Friday attendance premiums.

example.message: No salary records found for EmployeeId: emp-99
General settings not found.

put.description:
### 🔄 Dynamic Profile Synchronization

Modifies an existing employee record in the Identity store and HR database. This endpoint supports **Partial Updates**, meaning only fields included in the request body will be overwritten.

#### **⚙️ Update Intelligence:**
| Field | Logic Applied |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Null Guard** | Fields sent as `null` or omitted are ignored, preserving existing database values. |
| **Identity Sync** | Updating `Name` automatically synchronizes both `Name` and `UserName` properties. |
| **Enum Validation** | The `Gender` string is strictly parsed against the system's `Gender` enum. |

#### **⚠️ Constraints & Errors:**
* **404 Not Found**: Returned if the `Id` does not match any user in the **ASP.NET Identity** system.
* **400 Bad Request**: Triggered by invalid model state or unsupported `Gender` values.
* **500 Server Error**: Occurs if the Identity `UpdateAsync` operation fails (e.g., duplicate email check).

example.message: User updated successfully.
Invalid user data provided.
errors: The Email field is not a valid e-mail address.

example.message: User with ID 'emp-uuid-123' not found or could not be updated.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

put.summary: Manually override employee net salary for a specific period
put.description:
### 💰 Manual Payroll Correction

Allows authorized administrators to manually adjust the final net salary record for an employee. This operation targets a specific billing cycle defined by the `Month` and `Year` parameters.

#### **⚙️ Adjustment Intelligence:**
| Logic | Description |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Temporal Locking** | The update only affects the record matching the exact `Month` and `Year` in the `EmployeeSalaries` ledger. |
| **Non-Negative Guard** | Rejects any attempt to set a negative salary amount. |
| **Direct Injection** | Bypasses standard system calculations (Overtime/Absence) for that specific record. |

#### **⚠️ Operational Constraints:**
* **404 Not Found**: Returned if no salary record has been generated yet for the specified period.
* **Authorization**: Requires `Admin` privileges to maintain fiscal integrity.

example.message: Salary updated successfully.
Net salary cannot be negative.
Employee not found.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

put.description:
### ⏱️ Work Statistics Correction Hub

This administrative endpoint allows for the direct modification of hour logs for absences, vacations, overtime, or lateness. It serves as an override mechanism for automated system logs.

#### **⚙️ Processing Logic:**
| Category | Target Table | Logic |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Absent** | `EmployeeAbsents` | Filters by `EmployeeId` and exact `Date`. |
| **Vacation** | `EmployeeVacations` | Updates hours for approved vacation entries. |
| **Overtime/Late** | `EmployeeExtraAndLateHours` | Targets specific behavioral logs based on the `Type` string. |

#### **⚠️ Critical Rules:**
* **Temporal Precision**: The system matches the record based on the Day, Month, and Year components of the provided `Date`.
* **Type Rigidity**: Only the four specified types are processed; others return a 400 error.
* **Record Existence**: This is an **Update** operation; it will fail with 404 if a record for that date doesn't already exist.

example.message: Record updated successfully.
Invalid Type. Must be 'Absent', 'Vacation', 'Overtime', or 'LateHours'.
Record not found.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

delete.summary: Permanently delete a user and all related work records
delete.description:
### ⚠️ Irreversible Account Termination

Performs a multi-stage deletion process to remove a user from the system. This endpoint ensures that no residual data remains in junction tables.

#### **⚙️ Atomic Cleanup Workflow:**
| Phase | Target | Logic |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **1. Relationship Cleanup** | `EmployeeShifts` | Removes all links between the employee and their work schedules. |
| **2. Resource Cleanup** | `Shifts` | Deletes the specific shift configurations associated with this employee. |
| **3. Identity Deletion** | `AspNetUsers` | Finalizes the process by deleting the core Identity account. |

#### **🛡️ Transaction Integrity:**
* **Rollback Guard**: If any stage fails (e.g., Identity error), the entire transaction is rolled back, preserving all records.
* **Concurrency**: Logs a warning if the user is not found, returning a clean 404 response.

example.message: User and associated records deleted successfully.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.
User with ID 'guid-123' not found or could not be deleted.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

put.summary: Update or create additional salary incentives for an employee
put.description:
### 📈 Incentive & Commission Management

Manages the `AdditionalSalary` record for a specific employee. This endpoint allows administrators to inject sales-based bonuses and special Friday work rates into the payroll engine.

#### **⚙️ Upsert Intelligence:**
| Scenario | Action | Technical Result |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **No existing record** | **Create** | Inserts a new `AdditionalSalary` entry for the specified Month/Year. |
| **Record exists** | **Update** | Modifies the current `SalesPercentage` and `FridaySalary` values. |

#### **⚖️ Financial Rules:**
* **Temporal Scope**: The incentives are strictly tied to the provided `Month` and `Year` and will be picked up by the next salary calculation run.
* **Defaulting**: If `FridaySalary` is not provided, it defaults to `0` to prevent calculation errors.

example.message: Sales percentage updated successfully.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.
Employee not found.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

post.summary: Submit employee check-in with GPS verification
post.description:
### 📍 Precision Attendance Engine

This endpoint validates and records employee attendance using a multi-layered verification process. It ensures the employee is physically present and checking in within their assigned shift window.

#### **⚙️ Verification Pipeline:**
| Layer | Validation Logic |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Geo-Fencing** | Calculates the distance between the employee's coordinates and the branch center point. Must be within the branch `Radius`. |
| **Shift Matching** | Identifies the correct shift (including overnight support). Allows check-in **30 mins before** start and up to **1 hour before** end. |
| **Lateness Calculation** | Automatically triggers a 'Late' record if the check-in is more than **15 mins** after the shift start. |
| **Duplicate Prevention** | Prevents multiple check-ins for the same shift instance. |

#### **🕒 Time Handling:**
* **Overnight Support**: Seamlessly handles shifts that cross the midnight boundary.
* **Immutable Records**: Once a successful check-in is logged, it cannot be modified by the user.

example.message: Employee is not within the branch's designated area.
Attendance must be recorded between 30 minutes before the shift starts and 1 hour before the shift ends.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

post.summary: Record employee check-out and finalize shift
post.description:
### 🏁 Shift Termination & Overtime Engine

This endpoint finalizes the working session for an employee. It performs a reverse-lookup to match the check-out time with the previously recorded check-in.

#### **⚙️ Business Rules & Workflow:**
| Rule | Validation Logic |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Attendance Check** | **Strict Dependency**: User cannot record a leave unless a matching `Attendance` record exists for the current shift. |
| **Location Tracking** | Verifies the employee is still within the branch's GPS `Radius` during check-out. |
| **Shift Alignment** | Handles complex transitions, including **Overnight Shifts** and matching with the `NextShift` window. |
| **Overtime Processing** | Automatically triggers the `RecordOvertimeForDay` logic to calculate extra hours based on `GeneralSettings`. |

#### **⚠️ Operational Constraints:**
* **Duplicate Leave**: Prevents recording multiple leave entries for the same shift instance.
* **Time Range**: Leave must be recorded between the shift end time and the start of the next assigned shift.

example.message: Leave recorded successfully.
Employee is not within the branch's designated area.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

post.description:
### 🛡️ Authorized Attendance Correction

This endpoint allows administrators to manually record a check-out (leave) for an employee. It is primarily used for compliance corrections or when an employee is unable to check out via the mobile app.

#### **⚙️ Automated Simulation Logic:**
Unlike standard check-outs, this process auto-populates critical data to ensure a valid record:
| Data Point | Source of Simulation |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Geofencing** | Automatically uses the **Branch's Latitude/Longitude** coordinates to ensure the record passes location audits. |
| **Timestamp** | Combines the provided `LeaveDate` with the assigned **Shift End Time** from the database. |
| **Validation** | Verifies the employee-shift relationship before persistence. |

#### **⚠️ Security & Audit:**
* **Admin Only**: Strictly restricted to users with the `Admin` role.
* **Audit Trail**: This operation is logged as an administrative action for transparency.

example.message: Invalid input data.
Employee does not exist or is not assigned to this shift.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

post.summary: Create and assign a new work shift
post.description:
### 📅 Workforce Scheduling Engine

Creates a master shift record and immediately links it to a specific employee. This operation is wrapped in a **Database Transaction** to ensure both records are created successfully or none at all.

#### **⚠️ Error Scenarios & Business Rules:**
| Status Code | Scenario | Reason |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **201 Created** | **Success** | Shift created and assigned to employee successfully. |
| **404 Not Found** | **Employee Missing** | The provided `EmployeeId` does not exist in the database. |
| **400 Bad Request** | **Invalid Duration** | Shift duration cannot be zero or exceed 24 hours. |
| **400 Bad Request** | **Format Mismatch** | Time string does not match the required `h:mm` format. |

#### **⚙️ Scheduling Intelligence:**
| Logic | Description |
| :--- | :--- | 
| **Transaction Scope** | Uses `IDbContextTransaction` to maintain ACID compliance during multi-table inserts. |
| **Overnight Detection** | Automatically detects if the `EndTime` is less than `StartTime` and calculates the 24-hour duration correctly. |
| **Format Validation** | Strictly enforces the `h:mm` format (e.g., 8:30 or 20:45) using `TimeSpan.TryParseExact`. |

#### **📋 Operational Requirements:**
* **Authorization**: Requires a valid JWT Token with `Admin` role privileges.
* **Concurrency**: Transaction ensures that no orphaned shift records are created if the assignment fails.

application/json.example: Employee with the provided ID was not found in the system.
Invalid shift duration: Shift cannot be zero or exceed 24 hours.

example.message: You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

put.description:
### 🔄 Shift Synchronization & Validation

Updates the timing constraints for an existing shift record. The engine performs real-time duration recalculation to prevent illogical scheduling.

#### **⚙️ Update Intelligence:**
| Logic Point | Description |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Partial Updates** | If `StartTime` or `EndTime` is null, the system retains the existing database value. |
| **Duration Guard** | After merging new values, the system validates that the shift is between **1 minute and 24 hours**. |
| **Format Rigidity** | Time strings must strictly adhere to the `h:mm` 24-hour protocol. |

#### **⚠️ Error Handling Matrix:**
* **404 Not Found**: Returned if the `Id` does not match any existing master shift.
* **400 Bad Request**: Triggered by invalid time formats or if the resulting duration is zero/negative.

application/json.example: Invalid shift duration: Shift cannot be zero or exceed 24 hours.
Shift not found or update failed.

example.message: You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

delete.summary: Remove shift assignment and master record
delete.description:
### 🗑️ Atomic Shift Deletion

Permanently removes a work shift and its association with an employee. This is a critical administrative action protected by database transactions.

#### **⚙️ Deletion Workflow:**
| Step | Logic | Impact |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **1. Verification** | Validates existence of both `Shift` and `EmployeeShift` records. | Prevents partial or incorrect deletions. |
| **2. Transaction** | Initiates `BeginTransactionAsync`. | Ensures database consistency. |
| **3. Cascaded Delete** | Removes records from `Shifts` and `EmployeeShifts` tables. | Cleans up master and junction data. |

#### **🔐 Security & Constraints:**
* **Admin Privileges**: Only users with the `Admin` role can invoke this destructive operation.
* **Audit Alert**: Failed deletion attempts are logged with detailed warnings in the system telemetry.

example.message: You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

application/json.example: Shift with ID 12 for EmployeeId emp-9912-guid not found or deletion failed.

example.message: You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

get.summary: Retrieve assigned shifts for a specific employee
get.description:
### 📅 Work Schedule Retrieval

Fetches all active shift assignments associated with an employee. This endpoint is used to populate calendar views or shift-selection lists in the mobile application.

#### **⚙️ Data Formatting Logic:**
| Field | Transformation | Format |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **StartTime** | Strips Date, keeps 24h Time | `H:mm` (e.g. 9:00) |
| **EndTime** | Strips Date, keeps 24h Time | `H:mm` (e.g. 17:30) |

#### **🔐 Access Levels:**
* **Employees**: Can query their own schedule to verify attendance windows.
* **Administrators**: Use this to audit and manage workforce distribution.

application/json.example: No shifts found for EmployeeId emp-7721-v4.

get.summary: Fetch detailed information for a specific shift
get.description:
### 🔍 Individual Shift Inspection

Retrieves the comprehensive details of a single work shift by its unique database identifier. This includes the localized time formatting and the associated employee's identity GUID.

#### **⚙️ Retrieval Intelligence:**
| Process | Description |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Validation** | Rejects any ID less than or equal to zero before querying the DB. |
| **Relational Mapping** | Automatically scans the `EmployeeShifts` junction table to identify the linked employee. |
| **Time Localization** | Converts the internal `DateTime` storage into a clean `H:mm` string format using `InvariantCulture`. |

#### **⚠️ Operational Notes:**
* **Admin Only**: This endpoint is restricted to administrative auditing.
* **Data Integrity**: If a shift exists but is not yet assigned to an employee, the `EmployeeId` field will return an empty string.

example.message: You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

post.summary: Register a new public holiday in the corporate calendar
post.description:
### 🗓️ Corporate Calendar Governance

Adds a new fixed holiday to the system. These dates are automatically excluded from absence calculations and considered paid non-working days for all employees.

#### **⚙️ Validation Logic:**
| Phase | Action | Purpose |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Format Check** | `DateTime.TryParse` | Validates that the `VacationDay` string is a legitimate calendar date. |
| **Persistence** | Unit of Work Sync | Commits the record via Generic Repository to the `OfficialVacations` table. |
| **Formatting** | Date Normalization | The response returns the date in `yyyy/MM/dd` format for frontend consistency. |

#### **⚠️ Admin Note:**
Ensure the date provided does not conflict with existing records. This action requires **Admin** privileges.

example.message: Official vacation added successfully.

get.summary: Retrieve all programmed official holidays
get.description:

Fetches the complete list of non-working days defined by the organization. These dates impact attendance calculations and payroll cycles.

#### **⚙️ Technical Workflow:**
| Step | Logic |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Data Retrieval** | Uses Generic Repository to fetch all records from `OfficialVacations` table. |
| **DTO Mapping** | Converts `DateTime` objects to `yyyy/MM/dd` strings for standardized JSON output. |
| **Validation** | If the list is empty, a `404 Not Found` response is triggered to inform the UI. |

#### **🛡️ Security:**
* **Admin Only**: Restricted to Administrative roles to ensure calendar integrity.

get.summary: Retrieve a specific public holiday by its unique ID
get.description:
### 🔍 Single Holiday Lookup

Fetches detailed information about a registered official vacation. This is typically used before editing a record to ensure the correct data is populated.

#### **⚙️ Retrieval Logic:**
| Logic | Execution |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Input Validation** | Rejects any ID <= 0 with an ArgumentException to prevent unnecessary database hits. |
| **Data Sync** | Direct lookup via `GenericRepository.GetById`. |
| **Result Handling** | Returns a structured JSON response if found; otherwise, triggers a standard 404 response. |

#### **🛡️ Constraints:**
* **Security**: Accessible only to **Admin** users.
* **Format**: The date is strictly returned as a `yyyy/MM/dd` string.

example.message: Official vacation retrieved successfully.

put.summary: Modify an existing official holiday record
put.description:
### 🔄 Record Synchronization

Updates the metadata of a public holiday. This endpoint is crucial for adjusting floating holidays (like lunar-based Eids) or fixing clerical errors.

#### **⚙️ Update Intelligence:**
| Logic | Technical Rule |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Null Safety** | Rejects null models or IDs <= 0 before database context execution. |
| **Persistence Check** | Verifies existence using `GenericRepository`. Throws `KeyNotFoundException` if the record is missing. |
| **Parsing** | Enforces `DateTime.TryParse` to ensure the new date is logically valid. |
| **Partial Update** | Only fields provided with non-null values will be updated in the database. |

#### **⚠️ Impact Note:**
Updating a vacation date may trigger re-calculations in attendance reports for employees assigned to work during the newly defined holiday period.

example.message: Official vacation updated successfully.
Invalid vacation day format. Please provide a valid date (e.g., 'yyyy-MM-dd').
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.
You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.

delete.summary: Permanently remove an official holiday from the system
delete.description:
### 🗑️ Calendar Record Purge

Deletes a specific holiday record. This action is critical and will immediately impact the Attendance Engine's holiday validation logic for the affected date.

#### **⚙️ Deletion Workflow:**
| Stage | Action | Logic |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **1. Param Check** | ID Validation | Rejects IDs <= 0 via `ArgumentException`. |
| **2. Existence Check** | Record Lookup | Queries the database to ensure the holiday exists before attempting `Delete`. |
| **3. Execution** | Hard Delete | Removes the record and commits changes via `UnitOfWork.Save()`. |

#### **⚠️ Critical Impact:**
* **Attendance Re-calculation**: Days previously marked as holidays will revert to normal working days, potentially marking employees as 'Absent' if they don't check in.
* **Authorization**: Strictly limited to **Admin**.

204.description: Official vacation deleted successfully (No body returned).

example.message: You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.
No official vacation found with ID 15.
An error occurred while deleting the official vacation.
example.error: Vacation ID must be a positive integer.

get.summary: Check if a specific date is a non-working day
get.description:
### 📅 Holiday Verification Engine

Determines the operational status of any given date by cross-referencing global system rules and the corporate holiday registry.

#### **⚙️ Verification Logic (Priority Order):**
1. **Static Rule**: Automatically confirms any **Friday** as an official vacation.
2. **Dynamic Rule**: Queries the `OfficialVacations` table for matches on the provided date.

#### **🔬 Use Cases:**
* **Payroll Pre-check**: Verify if an employee's absence on a certain day should be penalized.
* **Shift Planning**: Ensure no mandatory shifts are assigned on holidays unless required.

#### **⚠️ Technical Note:**
The endpoint expects an ISO 8601 date format. Providing a default or empty date string will trigger a validation error.

example.message: Date 2026-03-20 is an official vacation.

get.summary: Generate a summary report of overtime hours for all employees
get.description:
### 📊 Overtime Analytical Report

Aggregates total extra working hours recorded in the `EmployeeExtraAndLateHours` table for the past 30 days. This report is essential for payroll processing and workforce efficiency auditing.

#### **⚙️ Reporting Intelligence:**
| Logic | Technical Execution |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Temporal Scope** | Automatically filters records from `DateTime.Now.AddMonths(-1)` to `Today`. |
| **Aggregation** | Groups data by `EmployeeId` to provide a single summed `TotalHours` per person. |
| **Data Sourcing** | Joins the `Users` table to retrieve human-readable names for reporting. |

#### **🛡️ Access Policy:**
* **Restricted**: This endpoint is strictly reserved for users with the **Admin** role to maintain salary and performance confidentiality.

example.message: You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.
An unexpected error occurred while retrieving the overtime report.

get.summary: Generate a summary report of net late hours for all employees
get.description:
### 🕒 Lateness Analytical Engine

Calculates accumulated late hours by comparing actual attendance timestamps against assigned shift start times. This report applies specific business thresholds to filter out minor delays.

#### **⚙️ Algorithmic Logic:**
| Factor | Rule / Value |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Late Threshold** | **1 Hour** (Hardcoded in service). Only delays exceeding this are calculated. |
| **Time Range** | Last 30 days (`AddMonths(-1)` to `Today`). |
| **Shift Discovery** | Joins `Attendances` with `EmployeeShifts` and `Shifts` to identify the target start time. |

#### **🔬 Calculation Formula:**
* **Net Late Hours** = `(AttendanceTime - (ShiftStartTime + 1 Hour))`. 
* Records are only summed if they fall within a valid window (from 30 mins before shift up to 24 hours after).

#### **🛡️ Access Policy:**
* **Admin Only**: Restricted for HR and Management oversight to ensure attendance policy compliance.

example.message: You must record attendance for the shift starting at 08:00 before requesting a leave.
An unexpected error occurred while retrieving the late report.

get.summary: Get detailed daily/monthly attendance logs for employees
get.description:
### 📋 Comprehensive Workforce Log

Generates a multi-layered report that pairs check-ins with check-outs and attaches branch/financial metrics. Supporting both Admin oversight and User self-service.

#### **⚙️ Intelligent Filtering:**
| Scenario | Result |
| :--- | :--- |
| **ReportType = 0 (Daily)** | Filters specifically for the date provided in `DayDate`. |
| **ReportType = 1 (Monthly)** | Filters by the provided `Month`. Defaults to current month if null. |
| **Custom Range** | Uses `FromDate` and `ToDate` if no ReportType matches. |

#### **🛡️ Critical Business Rules:**
* **Shift Pairing**: Logic uses `ShiftId` to ensure attendance and leave are from the same shift instance.
* **Geo-Aware**: Pulls `BranchName` directly from the employee's assigned headquarters.
* **Pagination**: Enforced to prevent server timeout on large date ranges.

get.summary: Generate a comprehensive monthly performance and attendance summary
get.description:
### 📈 Payroll-Ready Monthly Audit

This endpoint acts as the final data aggregator for payroll processing. It calculates the net contribution of each employee by analyzing their entire monthly lifecycle.

#### **⚙️ Intelligence Workflows:**
| Metric | Logic Applied |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Working Hours** | Sophisticated pairing of `Attendance` and `Leave` events. Supports multiple shifts per day. |
| **Absence/Vacation** | Direct distinct count from `EmployeeAbsents` and `EmployeeVacations` tables. |
| **Adjustments** | Pulls pre-calculated `Late` and `Overtime` hours for accurate summing. |

#### **🔬 Technical Note on Work Hours:**
The engine calculates hours by ordering daily events chronologically. For every `Attendance` mark, it seeks the subsequent `Leave` mark to close the shift duration. Unclosed shifts (missing Leave) are not counted in working hours to maintain financial integrity.

#### **🛡️ Authorization:**
* **Admin Only**: Due to the sensitive nature of salary-related metrics.

* **Identity Mapping**: Automatically resolves employee names and emails via the Identity store.
* **Pagination**: Ordered by `EmployeeId` with configurable `PageSize` to handle large workforce data.

post.description:
### ⚙️ System Policy Configuration

Establishes the foundational rules for payroll and attendance calculations. These settings are applied globally across all branches and employees.

#### **⚠️ Critical Validation Rules:**
| Field | Validation Logic | Impact |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Vacation Balance** | Must be >= 0. | Determines total yearly time-off allowance. |
| **Hourly Rate** | Must be > 0. | Direct multiplier for Overtime pay and Late penalties. |
| **Working Hours** | Must be > 0. | Defines the baseline for 'Full Day' attendance. |

#### **🛡️ Security Note:**
This endpoint is strictly locked to **Admin** users. Unauthorized attempts will return a `403 Forbidden` status.

get.summary: Retrieve current global business rules and payroll constants
get.description:
### 📖 Policy Reference Lookup

Provides the active configuration for the entire HR suite. These constants directly feed into the **Compensation & Leave** calculations.

#### **⚙️ Retrieval Logic:**
| Factor | Behavior |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Scope** | Global. Fetches the first available record from the `GeneralSettings` table. |
| **Safety** | If no record exists, an `InvalidOperationException` is logged and a `404` is returned. |
| **Usage** | Vital for UI components that need to display 'Allowed Vacations' or 'Work Hour' limits. |

#### **🛡️ Authorization:**
* **Admin Only**: Restricted to ensure organizational policy confidentiality.

put.summary: Update active global business rules and payroll constants
put.description:
### 🔄 Policy Re-calibration

Overwrites the current system-wide settings. Note that changes here will affect all subsequent salary and attendance calculations.

#### **⚙️ Business Rules Enforcement:**
| Rule | Logic | Exception if Fails |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Non-Negative Vacations** | `NumberOfVacationsInYear >= 0` | `ArgumentException` (400) |
| **Positive Rate** | `RateOfExtraAndLateHour > 0` | `ArgumentException` (400) |
| **Positive Work Hours** | `NumberOfDayWorkingHours > 0` | `ArgumentException` (400) |
| **Pre-existing Data** | Checks if any settings record exists. | `InvalidOperationException` (404) |

#### **🛡️ Authorization:**
* **Admin Only**: Unauthorized attempts (non-admins) will be rejected by the identity provider.

example.message: General settings updated successfully.

post.description:
### 🔔 Internal Messaging & Alerts

Dispatches a notification to a specific employee. This is widely used by the **Attendance Engine** to flag missing timestamps or shift discrepancies.

#### **⚙️ Service-Level Logic:**
| Logic | Action |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Title Enforcement** | The system automatically sets the title to **'Forget Leave Notification'** regardless of input. |
| **Timestamping** | `CreatedAt` is automatically assigned using `DateTime.Now` on the server. |
| **Persistence** | Data is saved to the `Notifications` table via Generic Repository. |

#### **📡 Integration Note:**
This endpoint is often called internally by background workers auditing the attendance logs at the end of each shift.

example.name: Ahmed Ali
example.message: Your attendance record is missing an exit timestamp.

get.summary: Retrieve a list of all system notifications for administrators
get.description:
### 📋 Admin Notification Hub

Fetches all generated alerts, primarily focusing on shift discrepancies and 'Forget Leave' incidents. 

#### **⚙️ Technical Logic:**
| Logic | Execution |
| :--- | :--- |
| **De-duplication** | Applies a custom `Distinct` comparer to ensure no redundant alerts are displayed for the same event. |
| **Data Sourcing** | Pulls from the `Notifications` table via Generic Repository and maps to `NotificationDTO`. |
| **Error Handling** | Returns an empty list `[]` instead of failing if a service-level exception occurs. |

#### **🛡️ Authorization:**
* **Restricted**: Only accessible by users with the **Admin** role for auditing purposes.

example.title: Forget Leave Notification
example.name: Ahmed Ali
example.message: Missing exit timestamp for today's shift.

delete.summary: Permanently remove a notification by ID
delete.description:
### 🗑️ Record Maintenance

Deletes a specific notification from the system. This is typically used by admins to dismiss resolved alerts or clear old logs.

#### **⚙️ Execution Workflow:**
| Phase | Action | Result if Fails |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Lookup** | `GetById(id)` | Returns `404 Not Found`. |
| **Deletion** | `Repository.Delete` | Moves record to deletion state. |
| **Commit** | `UnitOfWork.Save()` | Persists change to database. |

#### **🛡️ Authorization:**
* **Admin Only**: Restricted to administrative accounts to ensure audit logs are not cleared by unauthorized users.

Bearer.description:
JWT Authorization header using the Bearer scheme. 

 Enter 'Bearer' [space] and then your token in the text input below.

Example: "Bearer 12345abcdef"
Bearer.name: Authorization

tags.name: 1. Authentication & Account
tags.description:
### 🔐 Identity & Access Management (IAM)

The central security backbone of the HRSystem, responsible for employee identity verification, secure session persistence, and role-based access control.

#### **🚀 Core Security Modules:**
| Module | Responsibility | Key Features |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Authentication** | Identity Validation | JWT Issuance, Auto-Admin Bootstrapping, & Secure Login. |
| **Password Recovery** | Account Restoration | SMTP-integrated Reset Tokens with URL Safety Encoding. |
| **Employee Context** | Profile Initialization | Returns Shift timings, Branch Geo-fencing, and Salary data upon login. |

#### **🛡️ Security Standards:**
* **JWT Bearer Protection**: All protected endpoints require a valid `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header.
* **Identity Core Integration**: Built on top of **ASP.NET Identity** for industry-standard credential hashing and security stamping.
* **Policy-Based Access**: Granular control over administrative vs. employee-level actions through system roles.

> **Pro Tip:** Successful authentication not only provides a token but also hydrates the client-side state with the employee's assigned **Geo-fencing** coordinates for attendance.

tags.name: 2. Personnel & Organization
tags.description:
### 👥 Workforce & Infrastructure Governance

The administrative hub for managing the global employee directory and the physical organizational structure of the company.

#### **🚀 Core Management Modules:**
| Module | Responsibility | Key Features |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Employee Lifecycle** | Personnel Dossiers | CRUD operations, Identity synchronization, and Base Salary anchoring. |
| **Branch Network** | Spatial Infrastructure | GPS coordinate management and defined **Geo-fencing** perimeters (Radius). |
| **Access Control** | Role Orchestration | Granular permission management between Administrative and Staff levels. |

#### **📍 Infrastructure Standards:**
* **Geospatial Integrity**: Every branch center-point is defined by precision Latitude/Longitude to support the Attendance Engine.
* **Atomic Profiles**: Employee records are linked to specific branches and default work shifts upon creation.

> **Engineering Note:** Any modification to branch coordinates or radius parameters will propagate immediately, affecting all attendance validations for assigned personnel.

tags.name: 3. Precision Attendance Engine
tags.description:
### ⏱️ Time-Tracking & GPS Governance

The high-precision analytical engine responsible for validating employee presence using geospatial verification and algorithmic shift-matching.

#### **🚀 Core Intelligence Infrastructure:**
| Feature | Technical Logic | Business Impact |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Geo-Fencing** | Real-time Haversine distance calculation against branch coordinates. | Eliminates proxy attendance and location fraud. |
| **Shift Auto-Discovery** | Dynamic matching logic that supports **Overnight (Cross-Midnight)** transitions. | Ensures accurate tracking for 24/7 operations. |
| **Lateness Auditor** | Automated 15-minute threshold detection with immediate 'Late Record' persistence. | Streamlines payroll deductions without manual review. |

#### **🏗️ Operational Capabilities:**
* **Immutable Check-ins**: Enforces a strict one-time entry rule per shift instance to ensure data integrity.
* **Check-out Dependencies**: Intelligent verification ensuring a check-out cannot exist without a verified check-in.
* **Administrative Correction**: Secure manual override tools for HR managers to adjust records for special cases.

> **Engineering Note:** The engine utilizes a 30-minute early-arrival window and a 60-minute late-departure cutoff to maintain high data cleanliness.

tags.name: 4. Compensation & Leave
tags.description:
### 💰 Payroll & Vacation Governance

The financial heartbeat of the HR system, managing the delicate balance between employee attendance and fiscal compensation.

--- 

#### **🚀 Core Operational Modules:**
| Module | Functional Scope | Key Outcomes |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Salary Matrix** | Base Salary + Overtime - Penalties | Automated Net Pay Calculation |
| **Leave Engine** | Official Holidays & Time-off Requests | Accurate Balance Deduction |
| **Fiscal Auditing** | Comprehensive Attendance Reports | Data-driven Financial Summaries |

#### **📌 Compliance Standards:**
* **Zero-Error Payroll**: Calculations are cross-referenced with real-time logs from the *Precision Attendance Engine*.
* **Statutory Alignment**: Official vacations are pre-configured to ensure legal holiday compliance.
* **Transparent Reporting**: Every deduction (Late) or incentive (Overtime) is logged with a clear audit trail.

> **Finance Note:** All financial calculations use high-precision decimals to ensure accuracy in currency conversion and final pay outputs.

tags.name: 5. System Core & Automation
tags.description:
### ⚙️ Governance Settings & Background Jobs

The central nervous system of the application, responsible for enforcing global policies and orchestrating automated workflows.

--- 

#### **🕹️ Administrative Control Center:**
| Component | Responsibility | Tech Stack |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Global Policy** | Lateness, absence, and working hour thresholds. | .NET Core Options Pattern |
| **Background Jobs** | Daily attendance auditing & auto-status updates. | **Hangfire** Server |
| **Internal Messaging** | System alerts, forget-leave notifications. | SignalR / Local Push |

#### **🤖 Automation Workflows:**
* **The Auditor**: A scheduled job that runs at `23:59` daily to flag missing check-outs as 'Forget Leave'.
* **Penalty Engine**: Automatically applies deductions based on the *Rate of Extra & Late Hour* defined in General Settings.
* **Event Dispatcher**: Triggers notifications to employees when their shift status is corrected by the system.

> **Engineering Note:** Background jobs are configured with a re-try policy to ensure data consistency even in case of transient database failures.
